name: Review requests with Microsoft Teams and Google Sheets end to end

parameters.formTitle: Review requests with Microsoft Teams and Google Sheets end t

parameters.content:
### New Task Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Title, Assignee, Duedate). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Prepare Approval Packet

### Prepare Approval Packet

Shapes the item: writes `requestId`, `summary`, `requestedBy`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Request Approval via Microsoft Teams

### Request Approval via Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Next: Hold for Decision

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Request Approval via Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Hold for Decision

Pauses the execution until the resume webhook is called — this is the approval/HITL gate.

Next: Approved?

### Approved?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.body?.decision || $json.decision }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Apply Approved Change (Google Sheets) · ②→ Record Decline + Notify Requester

### Apply Approved Change (Google Sheets)

Upserts a row into 'Task Tracker', matched on `taskId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Next: Join Decision Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Record Decline + Notify Requester

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Next: Join Decision Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Join Decision Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Sort Tasks Newest First

### Sort Tasks Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

# Review requests with Microsoft Teams and Google Sheets end to end

**Pattern:** Approval Workflow · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Review requests with Microsoft Teams and Google Sheets end to end. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual onboarding is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Task Form Submission → Prepare Approval Packet → Request Approval via Microsoft Teams → Hold for Decision → Approved? → Apply Approved Change (Google Sheets) → Join Decision Paths → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to approval workflow without manual effort
- Anyone automating onboarding in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The workflow pauses on a Wait node configured to resume via webhook — n8n's actual human-in-the-loop primitive, not a polling loop. The resume URL is embedded directly in the approval message so approving is one click, and the paused execution burns no compute for however long the decision takes.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a timeout to the Wait node so unanswered approvals auto-decline after N hours.
2. Change the approval channel and confirm the resume link still works.
3. Add a third outcome (e.g. 'needs changes') alongside approve/decline.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🙋 Human in the loop

The workflow pauses at the Wait node until a human posts a decision to the resume URL (included in the approval message). Approve → the action executes; anything else → recorded as declined. No timeout is set by default — add one under the Wait node's options if approvals go stale.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your onboarding process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
